_source: iNAV Configurator 9.0.2 — app-9.0.2/resources/app/.vite/renderer/main_window/assets/index-Cu-IIg1k.js updateStatusBar(). The 32-bit field from MSP_STATUS @14 is the OR of armingFlags (state bits) and armingDisableFlags (reason bits).
_note: Bit 2 (4)=ARMED, bit 4 (16)=SIMULATOR_MODE are state bits, not disable reasons. Bit 17 (BOXKILLSWITCH) was removed in iNAV 9.x. Bits 0-1, 3, 5 are unused.

2.name: ARMED
2.reason: The FC is currently ARMED. This is shown here for completeness — it is not an arming-prevention flag.
2.fix: Disarm the aircraft before making configuration changes.

4.name: SIMULATOR_MODE
4.reason: The FC is in simulator mode.
4.fix: Exit simulator mode before arming for real flight.

6.name: ARMING_DISABLED_GEOZONE
6.reason: A geofence zone is active that prevents arming in this location.
6.fix: Disable geofencing or move the aircraft to a permitted zone before arming.

7.name: ARMING_DISABLED_FAILSAFE_SYSTEM
7.reason: Failsafe is active. The FC has lost RC signal or a failsafe condition was triggered by the system.
7.fix: Check your receiver is powered, bound, and transmitting. Verify the UART port and protocol (SBUS/CRSF/etc.) in the Ports tab. Ensure the TX is on.

8.name: ARMING_DISABLED_NOT_LEVEL
8.reason: The board is not level at rest (roll/pitch outside the allowed threshold).
8.fix: Set the FC flat on a level surface and calibrate the accelerometer in the Setup tab of iNAV Configurator. Also check 'board_align_roll/pitch/yaw' if the FC is mounted at an angle.

9.name: ARMING_DISABLED_SENSORS_CALIBRATING
9.reason: Sensors (gyro/acc) are still calibrating on boot.
9.fix: Wait a few seconds for calibration to complete. Keep the aircraft still on a flat surface immediately after powering on.

10.name: ARMING_DISABLED_SYSTEM_OVERLOADED
10.reason: CPU load is too high. The scheduler is overloaded and cannot guarantee safe flight loop timing.
10.fix: Reduce enabled features (GPS, logging, OSD, telemetry) to lower CPU load. Check 'tasks' output. Consider a faster FC target.

11.name: ARMING_DISABLED_NAVIGATION_UNSAFE
11.reason: A navigation mode is active or configured but GPS is not ready (no 3D fix, or GPS not configured).
11.fix: Wait for a 3D GPS fix with 6+ satellites before arming with nav modes. If you don't have GPS, disable nav-related features and modes.

12.name: ARMING_DISABLED_COMPASS_NOT_CALIBRATED
12.reason: Compass (magnetometer) is detected but has not been calibrated.
12.fix: Calibrate the compass in the Setup tab of iNAV Configurator. If you don't have or need a compass, disable it with 'set mag_hardware = NONE'.

13.name: ARMING_DISABLED_ACCELEROMETER_NOT_CALIBRATED
13.reason: Accelerometer has not been calibrated, or calibration data is missing/corrupt.
13.fix: Place the aircraft level and click 'Calibrate Accelerometer' in the Setup tab of iNAV Configurator.

14.name: ARMING_DISABLED_ARM_SWITCH
14.reason: The ARM switch is not in the correct position, or no ARM mode is assigned to a switch.
14.fix: Toggle your ARM switch to the ARM position. If no ARM switch is configured, use assign_switch() or set_flight_mode('ARM', ...) to add one.

15.name: ARMING_DISABLED_HARDWARE_FAILURE
15.reason: A hardware sensor has been detected as failing or unavailable.
15.fix: Run read_sensors() to see which sensor is unhealthy. Check the sensor's wiring and connection. A factory reset (defaults) may help if EEPROM is corrupt.

16.name: ARMING_DISABLED_BOXFAILSAFE
16.reason: Failsafe mode is active via a switch (BOXFAILSAFE assigned to a switch that is currently active).
16.fix: Move the FAILSAFE switch to its off/inactive position, or remove the FAILSAFE mode assignment if it was assigned accidentally.

18.name: ARMING_DISABLED_RC_LINK
18.reason: No RC signal is being received. The receiver is not sending valid channel data.
18.fix: Check: (1) TX is on and bound, (2) receiver is powered, (3) correct serial protocol configured in Ports tab (SBUS/CRSF/etc.), (4) correct channel map ('rxfail' CLI command for details).

19.name: ARMING_DISABLED_THROTTLE
19.reason: Throttle channel is not at minimum position.
19.fix: Push the throttle stick all the way to the bottom before arming. Check 'min_throttle' setting matches your ESC's low-throttle PWM.

20.name: ARMING_DISABLED_CLI
20.reason: CLI mode is currently active. The FC will not arm while you are connected via CLI.
20.fix: Exit the CLI (type 'exit' or disconnect the MCP server). The FC will return to normal operation.

21.name: ARMING_DISABLED_CMS_MENU
21.reason: CMS (OSD menu system) is currently open.
21.fix: Exit the CMS menu on your OSD.

23.name: ARMING_DISABLED_ROLLPITCH_NOT_CENTERED
23.reason: Roll and/or pitch sticks are not centered. Some configurations require centered sticks to arm.
23.fix: Center your roll and pitch sticks. Check transmitter trim and stick calibration.

24.name: ARMING_DISABLED_SERVO_AUTOTRIM
24.fix: Wait for servo autotrim to complete, or toggle the AUTOTRIM switch off.

25.name: ARMING_DISABLED_OOM
25.reason: Out of memory — the FC has run out of heap memory.
25.fix: Disable features to reduce memory usage. Flash a clean firmware build. This usually indicates a serious configuration problem.

26.name: ARMING_DISABLED_INVALID_SETTING
26.reason: An invalid parameter setting was detected in the configuration.
26.fix: Check the CLI for 'Invalid setting' errors. Run 'diff all' and look for suspicious values. A 'defaults' followed by reconfiguration may be needed.

27.name: ARMING_DISABLED_PWM_OUTPUT_ERROR
27.reason: A PWM output has a configuration error (pin conflict, timer conflict, or missing servo/motor assignment).
27.fix: Check motor and servo output assignments in the Motors/Servos tab. Verify timer allocation in the Resource tab.

28.name: ARMING_DISABLED_NO_PREARM
28.reason: Prearm switch is required but not active. PREARM mode is configured but the prearm switch is not in the armed position.
28.fix: Activate the prearm switch before arming, or remove the PREARM mode assignment if it is not needed.

29.name: ARMING_DISABLED_DSHOT_BEEPER
29.reason: DShot beeper command is currently active, preventing arming.
29.fix: Wait for the beeper sequence to complete, or deactivate the beeper command.

30.name: ARMING_DISABLED_LANDING_DETECTED
30.reason: Landing detector is active — the FC thinks the aircraft just landed.
30.fix: Wait a moment for the landing detector to clear. If this persists on the bench, check landing detection settings.
